course_aims: To enable students to competently and critically engage in, innovate and coordinate sustainable business practices.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of the drivers of sustainable practices in contemporary business and key concepts in sustainability including principles of governance, corporate legitimacy, stakeholder engagement and disclosure.

Demonstrate an understanding of key aspects of strategy, planning and reporting in sustainable business practice, including the Global Reporting Initiative, the CERES Roadmap, various relevant standards and certification schemes and how to apply the concept of Materiality.

Demonstrate an understanding of how to estimate, monitor and manage inputs and outputs like water, energy, waste and emissions from organisations, products and services, particularly in the built environment.
Demonstrate an understanding of principles of sustainable procurement, lean supply chain management and sustainable design.

Demonstrate an understanding of how human rights and social justice principles feature in sustainable business practice, particularly in regards to securing licenses to operate, effective employee management and ethical engagement with stakeholders, notably vulnerable groups and indigenous people.
Demonstrate an understanding of the influence of the banking, insurance and investment sector on sustainable business practice expectations and standards.

assessment_summary.category: Essay/ Critique, Participation/ Student contribution, Reflection
assessment_summary.title: Strategic Evaluation & Reflective Synthesis (Individual)

assessment_details.title: Critical ESG Brief (Individual)
assessment_details.task_description: Length: 1,500–1,800 words Objective:Critically assess a real-world company’s ESG performance, with a focus on the comparative application of multiple frameworks (e.g., GRI vs. SASB vs. TCFD). Students must analyze materiality determination, climate and biodiversity disclosures, and implications for investor decision-making. Output: Executive-level ESG brief + critical analysis appendix. Assessment Criteria: Analytical depth, integration of frameworks, clarity, referencing, and professional tone. Adaptation for External Students: Students may use examples from their own organization (if permissible). No live presentation required. Optional video summary to enhance engagement. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (the assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date.
For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Group ESG Transition Strategy Presentation
assessment_details.task_description: Title: Designing a Net-Zero and Inclusive ESG Transition Strategy Format: 8–10 minute recorded video presentation using tools like Zoom, Teams, or Loom. Objective: Develop a complete ESG strategy for a real or hypothetical organization transitioning toward net-zero emissions, DEI, and circular practices. Include scenario modeling, stakeholder mapping, risks, and opportunities aligned with material ESG metrics. Assessment Criteria: Strategy innovation, realism, stakeholder inclusivity, data-driven insights, team collaboration, and professional delivery. Adaptation for External Students: Online team formation via Blackboard. Collaboration through Microsoft Teams or shared platforms. Recorded presentations are reviewed asynchronously by staff. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Strategic Evaluation & Reflective Synthesis (Individual)
assessment_details.category: Essay/ Critique, Participation/ Student contribution, Reflection
assessment_details.task_description: Title: Evaluating ESG Governance and Reflecting on Systemic Change Length: 2,000–2,200 words Objective: Write a critical evaluation of an organization’s ESG governance structure, use of digital ESG tools, and alignment with frameworks like TNFD or CSRD. Reflect on the system-wide barriers and your evolving role as a change agent in business sustainability. Output: Strategy critique (60%) + personal professional reflection (40%). Assessment Criteria: Critical insight, integration of course theory, originality, personal learning synthesis, and communication quality. Adaptation for External Students: Can use their employer (with anonymization if needed). Reflection can be written or audio-recorded for flexibility. Encourages integration with real-world work experience. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Week 1 - Introduction to Sustainable Business Introduction, Course Overview, and Drivers of Sustainable Business Practice
Week 2 - ESG Reporting Frameworks ESG Reporting, the GRI Standard, and Materiality Assessment
Week 2 - Group Work/Discussion on ESG assurance case studies
Week 3 - Public Holiday/No Class Holiday
Week 4 - Environmental Footprinting
Week 4 - Group Work/Discussion on Footprinting scope comparison
Week 5 - Sustainable Procurement
Week 5 - Group Work/Discussion on Procurement risk analysis in a food company
Week 6 - Circular Economy
Week 6 - Group Work/Discussion on LCA workshop: Circularity in product design
Week 7 - Social Justice, DEI & Indigenous
Week 7 - Group Work/Discussion on Inclusion policy review from Australian firms
Week 8 - Stakeholder Engagement & Social License
Week 8 - Group Work/Discussion on Social license case: Mining in Indigenous land
Week 9 - Green Finance
Week 9 - Group Work/Discussion on Sustainable investment rating
Week 10 - Nature & TNFD
Week 10 - Group Work/Discussion on Applying TNFD to agricultural sector
Week 11 - ESG Digital Tools
Week 11 - Group Work/Discussion on ESG software platforms
Week 12 - Risk & Governance
Week 12 - Group Work/Discussion on ESG governance
Week 13 - Wrap-Up & Integration

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
